page: Microsoft Azure D128lds_v7 is a compute-focused virtual machine belonging to the Dldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with two threads per core. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, including AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8. The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled, providing 7559 GB of local disk capacity with Premium Storage capability.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 145,373.21 and a PassMark CPU score of 128,741.43, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory read bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 10,709.34 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 657.87 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The server excels in database and key-value operations, achieving 196.23 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and top-tier PassMark database performance. However, its memory performance is more moderate, with a PassMark memory score in the middle 50%. Large-model LLM text generation also shows average performance at 4.58 tokens per second, though prompt processing remains strong.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D128lds_v7 optimizes resource density by pairing a massive 128-vCPU compute engine with a relatively lean 256.0 GB memory footprint. This low-memory configuration reduces the premium typically associated with high-RAM instances, making it a cost-effective choice for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory allocations. The inclusion of 7559 GB of local storage further enhances value for data-intensive operations. This server is highly suited for multi-threaded CPU-bound tasks, high-throughput web serving, database operations, and batch processing workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure D128lds_v7 is a compute-focused virtual machine from the Dldsv7 family, powered by a 128-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 64 physical cores and 256.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, optimizing cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads by minimizing memory overhead. The server features 7559 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory read bandwidth, and exceptional database throughput, including 196.23 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. However, memory-bound performance and large-model LLM text generation speeds are moderate. The D128lds_v7 is best suited for high-throughput database operations, multi-threaded batch processing, and compute-intensive web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128lds_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128lds_v7 is a 128-vCPU Intel Xeon server with 256 GB RAM and 7559 GB local storage, optimized for high-compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density 128-vCPU compute platform with bundled local storage, optimized for intensive multi-threaded processing and database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6973P-C processors
256.0 GB of system memory providing a lean 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
7559 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities
Top-tier multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Optimized for compute-heavy, multi-threaded, and high-throughput workloads
